commit.message:
fix: remove lexical from peerDependencies — bundled as dependency

lexical is an internal implementation detail. Users never touch the
Lexical API directly (no public types, no exported Lexical instances).
Making it a peer was wrong — it required users to install an extra
package they never use.

- Remove peerDependencies.lexical from @lexify/core
- Remove peerDependencies.lexical from @lexify/plugins
- Update install instructions in all READMEs and docs

Users now only need:
  pnpm add @lexify/core

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: handle footnote refs, inline HTML, and math events gracefully

Previously these events were silently dropped in consume_inline.
Now they render as visible text:
- Footnote references: rendered as [label] in plain text style
- Inline HTML: rendered with code styling so users see the markup
- Math events (InlineMath/DisplayMath): rendered with code styling
  as a graceful fallback when math rendering is not available

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix boolector cmake: use runtime cmake wrapper with policy version flag

Instead of patching CMakeLists.txt (which may miss files rebuilt by
configure.sh), create a cmake wrapper at /usr/local/bin/cmake that
always passes -DCMAKE_POLICY_VERSION_MINIMUM=3.5 to the real cmake.
This handles all invocations by configure.sh regardless of which
CMakeLists.txt is processed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: cap IBM QPU Grover iterations at k=1 to defeat hardware noise

With N≈64 unique nodes, k=floor(π/4·√64)=6 iterations × two 5-ctrl Toffoli
gates = ~300+ CNOTs per iteration. On noisy real QPU this destroys Grover
amplification — target appeared at 1.3% (below 1/64≈1.56% uniform floor).

k=1 gives theoretical ~13.5% probability at circuit depth=12 (was ~72+).
Even with gate noise halving that, ~7% still clears the 5% detection threshold.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: eliminate extra spacing between inline spans in link text

When text contains links, render_text_with_links uses horizontal_wrapped
with separate widgets per span. egui's default item_spacing adds visible
gaps between adjacent text spans. Setting item_spacing.x to 0 within the
horizontal_wrapped layout makes text+link spans flow together naturally.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: ignore RT scheduling integration tests on macOS CI

macOS CI runners (like Windows) lack RT scheduling capabilities,
causing FFB jitter, HID latency, missed ticks, fault injection,
acceptance, and benchmark tests to fail with histogram overflow
or timing violations.

Add #[cfg_attr(target_os = "macos", ignore = ...)] to all 7 tests
that already have Windows ignores for the same reason.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Feature/rest encounter and refactors (#21)

* Refactor resolve_ore_play to use deck_play_random

Replaces inline logic for picking a random ore card from hand and
moving it to discard with the generic deck_play_random function,
matching how combat and fishing disciplines handle their plays.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Extend HasDeckCounts trait with individual accessor methods

Change HasDeckCounts from returning &DeckCounts/&mut DeckCounts to
individual accessor methods (deck_count, hand_count, discard_count
and their _mut variants). This allows both CardCounts and DeckCounts
to implement the trait with flat fields.

Implement HasDeckCounts for: DeckCounts, CardCounts, OreCard,
FishCard, PlantCard, EnemyCardDef, and LibraryCard. This enables
player-owned cards to use the same generic deck operations
(deck_draw_random, deck_shuffle_hand, deck_play_random) as
encounter-internal cards.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add rest encounter type (roadmap 9.4)

Implement the rest encounter discipline with:
- Rest card types, definitions, and encounter state in types.rs
- Rest discipline module with card registration (3 templates -> 5 cards x 5 copies)
- start_rest_encounter and resolve_rest_card_choice on GameState
- Action handler integration for picking and playing rest encounters
- Rest encounters compose ~20% of encounter deck (hand: 4)
- Recovery effects apply stamina/health with caps, costs in fish/herbs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add scenario test for rest encounter full loop

Tests: new game → scout → pick rest encounter → verify 5 cards drawn →
pick cost-free card → verify encounter won → back to scouting.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update roadmap and vision docs for rest encounter

- Mark roadmap 9.4 as completed with implementation details
- Update roadmap 9.5 with light level cap and wood cost details
- Add Rest to vision.md EncounterState/EncounterKind enums
- Add Rest encounter type description and win pattern
- Update HasDeckCounts trait description with new accessor design

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update vision-roadmap-suggestions.md for rest encounter

Mark suggestions 51-52 as applied (HasDeckCounts + ore play-random).
Add new suggestions 57-63 from rest encounter implementation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Mark issues.md items as completed

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* BREAKING: Refactor rest deck to player deck with rest tokens

- Add RestToken and RestMaxHand to TokenType enum
- Add CardKind::Rest { effects, rest_token_cost } variant
- Remove old rest types: RestCard, RestDef, RestCardEffectTemplate,
  RestCostRange, RestRecoveryRange, ConcreteRestRecovery
- Simplify EncounterKind::Rest to unit variant (no encounter-internal deck)
- RestEncounterState now tracks rest_tokens instead of rest_hand
- Rest cards use ConcreteEffect/GainTokens pattern with PlayerCardEffect
  references, consistent with Attack/Defence/Resource cards
- Rest encounter draws cards from Library deck to hand using RestMaxHand
- Playing rest cards costs rest_token_cost from encounter pool and
  material costs (Fish/Plant) as percentage-of-gain from player tokens
- Encounter auto-completes when rest tokens are depleted
- Rest abort always results in PlayerWon (player cannot lose rest)
- Replay logic updated to handle rest card play internally

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update tests for rest deck refactoring

- Update library card count assertion: 55 → 64 (4 rest effects + 5 rest cards)
- Rewrite scenario_rest_encounter_full_loop for new rest flow:
  - Uses Library card IDs instead of index-based rest card selection
  - Validates rest_tokens on encounter state (1-2 range)
  - Plays cost-free rest card or aborts if none available
  - Verifies PlayerWon outcome for both play and abort paths

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Mark rest deck issues as completed in docs/issues.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update vision.md and roadmap.md for rest deck refactoring

- Document rest cards as player library cards with CardKind::Rest
- Document rest token mechanic (1-2 tokens, multi-card play)
- Document percentage-of-gain cost system for rest material costs
- Update encounter win/loss pattern from single-choice to multi-play
- Add RestToken and RestMaxHand to token reference
- Remove references to old RestCard/RestDef types

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update vision-roadmap-suggestions.md for rest deck refactoring

- Mark outdated suggestions (#57, #59, #62) as applied/superseded
- Add new suggestions #64-70: encounter-scoped token taxonomy,
  cost pipeline docs, CardKind readability, replay branching,
  cooking mechanic update, rest token progression, cost rebalancing

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: lock IBM QPU path to 6 qubits (1 codon)

4 qubits is architecturally impossible — 2 bits/nt × 3 nt = 6 qubits minimum
per codon. Multi-codon IBM circuits (12/18 qubits) are unreliable on noisy
free-tier QPU hardware.

- backend/models/schemas.py: BioIBMSubmitRequest n_codons le=3 → le=1
- frontend/src/components/GroverPOC.jsx: IBM submit hardcodes n_codons: 1

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Align dark mode statusbar with Zed theme

Use secondary (#212121) instead of tertiary (#494949) for the dark mode
statusbar background, matching zed-alpental-theme. Adds a dedicated
status_bg palette key so light and dark variants can diverge.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test(perf): reduce test durations and fix timeout flakes

- Reduce git-log heuristic test commits from 20 to 8 (still well above
  60% threshold) — cuts ~24 execSync calls per test
- Use deterministic filenames in initGitRepo instead of Date.now() random
- Increase --diff test timeout from 15s to 30s (matches global timeout)
- Increase --no-clean test timeout from 30s to 60s (runs two full syncs)

Total test duration reduced from ~163s to ~54s (66% improvement).
All 771 tests pass with 0 flakes.

Add Array.isArray check to prevent false "missing name/phase" warnings
when project.yaml parses as an array instead of an object.

commit.message:
refactor: redesign course page and fix theme architecture

- Rewrite course selection grid with flex layout and alternating row colors
- Add weekend toggle switch in course page app bar
- Sync period labels with grid scrolling
- Move themeNotifier to theme.dart to avoid circular imports
- Remove broken section.dart, apply bold title styling across pages

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: IBM QPU threshold-based detection — fixes NOT_FOUND on noisy 6-qubit hardware

Root cause: detection used strict equality (max(counts) == target_bits) which fails
on noisy real QPU hardware where gate errors shift the maximum result.

Changes:
- backend/routers/quantum.py: replace strict equality with IBM_DETECT_THRESHOLD=0.05
  (5% ≈ 3.2× uniform for 6 qubits). Fix field name user_email→email in find_one/
  update_one to match insert schema. Return null confidence when no DB record so
  frontend fallback activates correctly.
- frontend/src/components/GroverPOC.jsx: fix prev-out-of-scope ReferenceError
  (prev→ibmResult outside setIbmResult callback). Use backend detection_result/
  confidence when available; threshold fallback from counts otherwise.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: prevent scroll snap from side-by-side sync overriding nav targets

When the nav panel sends a scroll target and the mode is SideBySide,
sync_side_by_side_scroll was immediately overriding the nav target on
the same frame, causing a visible snap. Added a per-frame guard
(nav_scroll_applied_this_frame) that prevents the sync from firing
on frames where a nav scroll was already applied.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: add missing DELETE RLS policy for actividad table

Activities were not being deleted because there was no DELETE policy
on the actividad table (RLS silently blocks with 0 rows affected).
Added actividad_delete policy for admin/supervisor roles.
Also added client-side detection of 0-row deletes.

IMPORTANT: Run this SQL in Supabase SQL Editor:
  CREATE POLICY actividad_delete ON actividad
  FOR DELETE USING (is_admin_or_supervisor());

commit.message:
feat: Ed25519 trust store implementation

- Add fail-closed mode to TrustStore: new_fail_closed() and
  open_or_fail_closed() constructors ensure all lookups return
  Distrusted when the trust store cannot be loaded (safety-critical)
- Add is_key_trusted() convenience method for common trust checks
- Add add_key_from_hex() for hex-encoded public key import
- Make get_trust_level() and get_public_key() respect fail-closed flag
- Document placeholder default key ([0u8; 32]) as development-only
- Add comprehensive tests: real Ed25519 sign/verify round-trip,
  tampered content rejection, distrusted key rejection, unknown key
  handling, fail-closed behavior, hex key operations
- Apply same changes to service crate trust store for consistency

commit.message:
feat: capability badges in model selectors + text-based tool call interception (#133)

* feat: intercept text-based tool calls from small models

Small models (1-8B) often emit tool calls as plain JSON text instead of
using the structured tool calling protocol. Detect this pattern, parse
the JSON, resolve the kernel function, invoke it, and re-prompt the
model with the result so it produces a natural-language response.

Handles both non-streaming and streaming paths. Gracefully ignores
non-tool JSON or parse failures.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: show capability badges and legend in all model selectors

Add capability badges (💬=Chat 🔧=Tools 👁=Vision 📐=Embed) to the
startup model selector that was previously missing them. Add a legend
to both the startup selector and /model picker so users understand
what each icon means.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: improve table column width distribution and add horizontal scroll

- Two-pass column width normalization: first scale, then clamp to minimum,
  then redistribute overflow among non-clamped columns. Prevents total
  width from exceeding available space when many columns hit minimum.
- Dynamic minimum column width: scales with body_size (2.5em, floor 36px)
  instead of hardcoded 40px.
- Horizontal scroll for wide tables: tables exceeding available width are
  wrapped in a horizontal ScrollArea instead of overflowing.
- Updated test helper to mirror the new algorithm.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: improve height estimation accuracy for viewport culling

- Blockquotes: account for bar margin + content margin (~1em + 3px)
  instead of flat 20px reduction
- Lists: compute content width from actual bullet column width
  (1.5em + 2px) and increase per-item spacing factor (0.2→0.3)
- Tables: add per-row spacing (4px) to account for egui Grid internals
- Code blocks: include language label height when a language tag is present
- Images: use viewport-aware estimate (75% of wrap_width for ~4:3 aspect)
  instead of fixed 10× body_size, with 8× body_size minimum floor

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
